articles.headline: Agentic AI: the future of space warfare
articles.blurb: The future of space warfare will be determined by the speed of decision. As satellite constellations proliferate and adversaries field increasingly sophisticated counterspace capabilities, the ability to sense, analyze and act faster than an opponent will dictate who controls the space domain....
articles.takeaway: Space domain control will shift from traditional military capabilities to autonomous AI decision-making speed, fundamentally reshaping orbital warfare doctrine and satellite constellation vulnerability.

articles.headline: Up To 16 Hours: British Airways' 10 Longest Nonstop Flights From London [2026]
articles.blurb: British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...
articles.takeaway: BA's expansion to 95 daily long-haul departures positions it to challenge top carriers' ultra-long-haul dominance despite ranking fifth globally.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents post-incident to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation.

articles.headline: More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East
articles.blurb: The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. articles.takeaway: USAF's persistent A-10 deployments signal sustained commitment to Iran operations despite aircraft's age, reflecting ongoing demand for specialized close-air-support capabilities in contested Middle Eastern theaters.

articles.headline: Trump International? One Final Hurdle Left For Palm Beach Airport's Renaming
articles.blurb: The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Safety Risk Management effectiveness depends on organizations systematically documenting why chosen controls are proportional to identified hazards, not just selecting the most obvious mitigation options.

articles.blurb: Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.
articles.takeaway: As quadjet operators dwindle, remaining airlines must justify high operating costs through premium long-haul routes and cargo conversions to maintain fleet viability.

articles.headline: China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation layers offers immediate efficiency gains while the industry waits for breakthrough propulsion technologies to mature.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Artemis 2 countdown underway
articles.blurb: The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.
